name: BluSnu
summary: Blu Snu: The Ultimate Offensive Bluetooth Framework

documentation.readme: Blu Snu: The Ultimate Offensive Bluetooth Framework
usage_sections: Standard Mode: Uses public Android APIs for scanning and GATT interaction.
Root Mode: leverages su access to execute low-level system commands, modify the Bluetooth stack, and perform raw HCI injection.
Hardware Mode: Interfaces with external USB dongles (via OTG) for promiscuous mode sniffing and jamming.
Dashboard: Real-time overview of the wireless landscape, tracking active devices and signal density.
Device Management: Detailed fingerprinting of targets, including MAC address, signal strength (RSSI), device class, and supported profiles (SDP/GATT).

arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: Blu Snu: The Ultimate Offensive Bluetooth Framework.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Blu Snu: The Ultimate Offensive Bluetooth Framework.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/hereliesaz/blusnu.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./gradlew assembleDebug`. Blu Snu: The Ultimate Offensive Bluetooth Framework.
